channel.title: Frontion News - Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇺🇸 Gunman opens fire at White House Correspondents Dinner, Trump evacuated safely
item.description: A shooter fired shots at the Washington Hilton during the White House Correspondents Association dinner, forcing President Trump and top officials to be evacuated. One officer was shot but saved by a bulletproof vest; the suspect is in custody.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US-Iran peace talks collapse as Trump cancels envoy visit to Pakistan
item.description: Diplomatic efforts stalled after Trump called off Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner trip to Islamabad, saying Iran offered a lot but not enough. Both sides show little willingness to soften terms as the war enters its third month.

item.title: 🇹🇳 Tunisia suspends one of Africas oldest human rights groups in widening crackdown
item.description: Authorities ordered a one-month suspension of the Tunisian League for Human Rights, part of the Nobel Peace Prize-winning National Dialogue Quartet. The move signals deepening authoritarianism in the North African nation.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇲🇲 Chinas top diplomat meets Myanmar junta leader on regional tour
item.description: Chinas Foreign Minister visited Naypyidaw and met with the military-backed government leader, deepening Beijings regional ties as Myanmars civil war continues. The visit underscores Chinas growing influence in Southeast Asia.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇫🇷 Macron says EU mutual defense clause is stronger than NATO amid US commitment doubts
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron declared the EUs Article 42.7 mutual defense clause stronger than NATO, as European leaders debate building independent defense capabilities. The statement reflects growing anxiety about Washingtons reliability.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Netanyahu Orders IDF Strikes on Hezbollah After Ceasefire Violations
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu ordered the IDF to "vigorously" attack Hezbollah in southern Lebanon after the group launched projectiles into northern Israel. The ceasefire extension announced by Trump appears increasingly fragile as both sides exchange fire.

item.title: 🇭🇺 Hungary's Orban Refuses Parliament Seat After Historic Election Defeat
item.description: Viktor Orban, Hungary's dominant political figure for 36 years, said he will not take up his parliament seat following his party's landslide defeat. He plans to lead Fidesz through a "renewal" process, with analysts seeing his loss as evidence that right-wing populism may be peaking.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israel Kills Four in Southern Lebanon Despite Ceasefire Extension
item.description: Israeli strikes killed at least four people in southern Lebanon's Nabatieh district, as Israel continues attacks despite a three-week ceasefire extension. Hezbollah lawmakers say the ceasefire is "meaningless" given Israel's ongoing hostilities.

item.title: 🇮🇳 India Signs $1.2 Billion Deal for Russian Long-Range Air-to-Air Missiles
item.description: India has reportedly signed a deal for 300 Russian R-37M ultra-long-range missiles designed to target support aircraft like airborne early-warning and tanker planes. The move comes as Pakistan and China continue integrating their aerial weapons systems.

item.title: ☢️ UN Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty Review Opens Amid Global War Tensions
item.description: Signatories to the landmark NPT meet at the UN as arms control agreements between major powers have collapsed and nuclear capabilities expand. The nine nuclear-armed states now possess over 12,000 warheads, with the US and Russia holding 90%.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US-Iran Ceasefire Talks Collapse as Trump Cancels Envoys Trip to Pakistan
item.description: The latest ceasefire negotiations between Washington and Tehran failed after Iran's foreign minister left Islamabad and Trump told his envoys not to travel. Trump said Iran needs to call if they want to talk, while Iran questioned how it can trust the US after its port blockade.

item.title: 🇫🇷🛢️ Macron Pushes to Reopen Hormuz as TotalEnergies Warns of Global Energy Shortages
item.description: French President Macron reiterated efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz after TotalEnergies CEO warned of global energy shortages if the Iran war continues for months. The waterway remains nearly blocked, disrupting oil and LNG shipments worldwide.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇵🇰 Trump Cancels Kushner and Witkoff Iran Mission After Tehran Offer Falls Short
item.description: President Trump cancelled his envoys' trip to Pakistan for Iran talks, saying Tehran 'offered a lot but not enough.' Iran had said no direct talks were planned and its foreign minister left Islamabad for Oman before heading to Russia.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ Republicans Recalibrate Midterm Strategy Amid Trump Approval Slump and Iran War
item.description: With gas prices up, Trump's approval ratings down, and the Iran war dragging on, Republicans are retooling their midterm election blueprint to focus on Trump's policies while distancing themselves from the president personally.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Cancels US Peace Delegation Trip to Pakistan as Iran Talks Falter
item.description: President Trump canceled a planned US delegation trip to Pakistan for peace talks with Iran, citing "infighting" in Tehran and the lack of a clear negotiation plan. The move deals a blow to diplomatic efforts to end the US-Iran conflict.

item.title: 🏛️ Trump Fires Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem
item.description: President Trump fired Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem following mounting criticism over her handling of immigration enforcement and disaster response. Senator Markwayne Mullin has been nominated as her replacement.

item.title: 💰 World's Largest Condom Maker Karex to Raise Prices Up to 30% Due to Iran War
item.description: Malaysia-based Karex, which produces over 5 billion condoms annually for brands like Durex and Trojan, announced price increases of up to 30% due to Iran war disruptions to oil-derived raw materials including ammonia and silicone lubricants.

item.title: 🛢️ Trump Vows No Hormuz Blockade Lift Until Iran Deal Reached
item.description: President Trump stated the US will not lift its naval blockade of Iranian ports until a deal is reached with Tehran. US Central Command reports 27 vessels have been turned away since the blockade began, while Iran maintains its own blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 🇮🇩 Acid Attacks Target Indonesian Activists Amid Growing Climate of Repression
item.description: Environmental and human rights activists in Indonesia face escalating attacks, including acid assaults, under President Prabowo's government which rights groups say is increasingly hostile to dissent.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Seeks Alternative Middle East Energy Routes to Bypass Hormuz
item.description: European Commission President von der Leyen announces plans to work with Gulf countries on new export infrastructure that avoids the Strait of Hormuz, as the bloc's energy bill has skyrocketed by €25 billion due to the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇨🇴 Colombia Highway Bomb Attack Kills 10 Ahead of Presidential Election
item.description: A highway bombing in the Cauca department killed 10 people and injured at least 12, with authorities blaming FARC dissidents. The attack comes amid a wave of violence before next month's presidential election.

item.title: 🇲🇱 Mali Declares Curfew After Coordinated Terrorist Attacks Hit Bamako
item.description: Authorities imposed a night curfew in the Gao region after coordinated attacks by terrorist groups targeted military sites in Bamako and other areas. The military claims hundreds of attackers were neutralized.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Former MIT President Warns US Is Losing Innovation Race to China
item.description: L. Rafael Reif, who led MIT from 2012 to 2022, argues in Foreign Affairs that America is ceding scientific leadership to China, pointing to advances in EVs, nuclear energy, and military technology.

item.title: ☢️ Ukraine Commemorates 40th Anniversary of Chornobyl Disaster Under Shadow of War
item.description: Ukraine marked the 40th anniversary of the Chornobyl nuclear disaster on Sunday amid ongoing fears that Russia's war could trigger another nuclear catastrophe. The Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant remains a concern.

item.title: 🇲🇱 Al Qaeda-Linked Militants Launch Major Coordinated Attacks Across Mali
item.description: JNIM, an al Qaeda affiliate, and Tuareg rebels claimed responsibility for coordinated attacks across Mali targeting the capital Bamako and military bases. Experts called it one of the boldest operations in years.
